title: Unable to get SRV or TXT records in podman container
body:
### Bug description

MongoDB introduced the concept of a seed list that is specified using DNS records, specifically SRV and TXT records. Please refer [this document](https://www.mongodb.com/developer/products/mongodb/srv-connection-strings/) for detail.
I tried to connect in this way in podman container, but it failed. I wrote a demo script to reproduce this issue.
This issue occurs on **podman-desktop for MAC**. But when I tried podman on RHEL8, it works.

### Operating system

macOS Ventura 13.4 (Intel)

### Version

1.0.x

### Steps to reproduce

Run nslookup in a alpine container

```shell
podman run -it --rm alpine nslookup -type=txt rs.joedrumgoole.com
```

### Relevant log output

```shell
Server:		192.168.127.1
Address:	192.168.127.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:

```

### Expected results

```shell
Server:		64.104.76.247
Address:	64.104.76.247#53

Non-authoritative answer:
rs.joedrumgoole.com	text = "authSource=admin&replicaSet=srvdemo"

Authoritative answers can be found from:

```

### Additional context

If I specified the DNS server, it also works. But this container can not connect to other containers.

```shell
$ podman run -it --rm --dns 8.8.8.8 alpine nslookup -type=txt rs.joedrumgoole.com
Server:		8.8.8.8
Address:	8.8.8.8:53

Non-authoritative answer:
rs.joedrumgoole.com	text = "authSource=admin&replicaSet=srvdemo"

```

comments.body: transfering the issue to podman tracker as the podman machine (using QEMU today) on macOS is managed by this project.

Seems to be a problem with the built in gvproxy dns. It doesn't seems to support any txt records.

Using `--dns` should work, I am not sure what you mean by this container cannot connect to other containers? If they are on the same network everything gets proxied through aardvark-dns which then will connect the dns server specified with `--dns`. This should work fine on the lasted version.
I filled https://github.com/containers/gvisor-tap-vsock/issues/233, it needs to be fixed there. There isn't much podman can do here so I close it in favour of the gvproxy issue.
